skill.name: claude-speed-reader
skill.description: Speed read Claude responses at 600+ WPM using RSVP with Spritz-style ORP highlighting.
skill.summary: Use RSVP speed-reading patterns to review long Claude responses faster.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found two hardcoded URLs and two high-severity weak-cryptography alerts in SKILL.md. The URLs are documentation links to a source repository, and the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives because the file contains no cryptographic operation, script, or executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links to External Repository
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE for hardcoded URL detection. SKILL.md contains source repository links at lines 4 and 22, but they are plain documentation references and do not execute network requests or transmit data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain visible repository URL references only. No code path, command, or automatic network access is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptographic algorithm detection. The flagged locations are descriptive text about speed reading and do not contain cryptographic functions, weak algorithm names, secrets, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both flagged lines are Markdown or frontmatter text describing the skill purpose. There is no implementation content where a cryptographic weakness could occur.

content.value_statement: Long AI responses can slow review and comprehension. This skill points users toward RSVP speed-reading patterns for faster review of Claude output.
actual_capabilities: Describes a Claude response speed-reading workflow based on RSVP presentation.
References Spritz-style optimal recognition point highlighting as the intended reading method.
Identifies when to use the workflow for faster review of long Claude responses.
Provides a source repository link for users who need more information.
Works as lightweight guidance without installing scripts or running commands.
limitations: The skill file does not include an embedded speed-reader application.
It does not provide setup steps, browser automation, or command-line tools.
It depends on the external source repository for implementation details.
It is focused on reading Claude responses, not summarizing or rewriting content.

use_cases.title: Review Long Answers Quickly
use_cases.description: Use the skill to guide faster reading of detailed Claude responses during research or analysis.

use_cases.title: Scan Implementation Guidance
use_cases.description: Use RSVP reading patterns to move through generated coding explanations with less manual scrolling.

use_cases.title: Process Meeting Notes
use_cases.description: Use the reading workflow to review Claude-generated summaries, notes, and action lists more efficiently.

prompt_templates.title: Start Speed Reading
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use with a long Claude response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me use the Claude speed reader workflow to review this long response at a comfortable pace.

prompt_templates.title: Adjust Reading Pace
prompt_templates.scenario: Tune the reading experience for comprehension.
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through reading this response with RSVP pacing. Start slower, then increase speed when the content becomes familiar.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Key Sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Read dense output while prioritizing important material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the speed-reading workflow for this response, but slow down around decisions, warnings, and action items.

prompt_templates.title: Review Technical Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for long technical responses.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the RSVP reading approach to this technical explanation. Preserve code terms, pause at section changes, and flag concepts that need rereading.

output_examples.input: A user wants to review a long Claude answer about project planning.
output_examples.output: The assistant frames the response for speed reading, identifies dense sections, and recommends slower pacing for decisions and deadlines.

output_examples.input: A developer needs to read a detailed implementation explanation.
output_examples.output: The assistant suggests using RSVP pacing, slowing around file names, commands, and risky changes.

output_examples.input: A team member wants to process a Claude-generated meeting summary.
output_examples.output: The assistant helps prioritize action items, owners, and dates while keeping the reading flow fast.

best_practices: Use lower reading speeds for technical, legal, or unfamiliar content.
Pause or reread sections that contain decisions, requirements, or warnings.
Use the source repository when you need implementation details beyond this guidance file.
anti_patterns: Do not use maximum speed when accuracy is more important than throughput.
Do not rely on speed reading as a substitute for verification.
Do not assume the skill installs or runs a reader by itself.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps users think about faster review of Claude responses using RSVP and ORP speed-reading concepts.

faq.question: Does it include a working speed reader?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains guidance and source links, not an embedded application.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Codex support, but the described workflow is centered on reading Claude responses.

faq.question: What is RSVP reading?
faq.answer: RSVP shows words in sequence so the reader can focus on one reading position at a time.

faq.question: What is ORP highlighting?
faq.answer: ORP highlighting marks a word position that can help the eye recognize words faster.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only documentation links and dismissed the weak-cryptography alerts as false positives.
